1.title: Curate: American Season
1.description:
**Should the ZAO fund this project?**

**Project:** American Season
**Link:** [View Project](https://artizen.fund/index/p/american-season?season=6)

American Season integrates AI agents as collaborative tools that help organize interviews, surface themes, and support ethical decision-making while keeping authorship human-led. The project operates within a creator-owned, fair-compensation model, using decentralized coordination to build with parents and communities transparently. It culminates in public gatherings and immersive activations rooted in grassroots cultural production.

Vote **Yes** to include or **No** to pass.

2.title: https://artizen.fund/index/p/gaias-sound-temple
2.description: The Gaia Sound Temple is a creator-owned, independently operated planetary listening network — distributing live sound streams from volcanic regions, ice caves, forests, and oceans into galleries, classrooms, and a public web interface worldwide. AI drives site architecture, adaptive educational tools, and environmental pattern recognition, woven into the backbone of the project as living infrastructure. The streaming network itself is a new kind of technological nervous system for the planet. Each installation location is governed by ethical agreements with local ecological stewards. Non-extraction is built into the governance from the ground up — every partnership with an ecosystem or community is rooted in relationship, not transaction. Community participation runs through every layer. The biological station network connects research stations across ecosystems. Schools tune into live biospheric audio as a planetary classroom. Artists recompose Earth sounds live in public performances and listening rituals, making the planet itself a co-performer. Live streams, an interactive global map, and open educational interfaces keep the infrastructure visible and accessible in real time. The project unfolds in public, continuously. Gallery installations project the live sky color of transmitting locations while biospheric audio fills the room — bringing the atmosphere of a Patagonian dusk or an Arctic coastline into physical space, felt and shared by anyone who walks in. The Gaia Sound Temple is collaborative cultural production at planetary scale, using technology to deepen our listening, expand ecological awareness, and invite collective care.

3.title: https://artizen.fund/index/p/international-artists-project?season=6
3.description: International Artists Project is creating International Artists Festival with music artists born in every country of the world, as a follow-up to The World Album, which was the first album to feature music artists from every country.

5.title: Curate: FEMSTAGE
5.description:
**Should the ZAO fund this project?**

**Project:** FEMSTAGE
**Link:** [View Project](https://artizen.fund/index/p/femstage?season=6)

Hi,my name is Zule Rojas and i am the founder of FEMSTAGE, a project dedicated to showcasing and connecting high-level professional women in the audiovisual industry. Femstage was created to provide support and opportunities for creative and technical female talent, in a sector where representation is still limited. Our platform brings together active, experienced professionals, offering:   - Connection between experts for collaborative projets. - Access to advanced training, specialized workshps, and legal and financial advisory - Support for technical and creative projets, promoting initiatives led by women with cultural and professional impact.  we believe FEMSTAGE reflects professionalism, stability, and innovation, bringing together established experts on a digital platform that enhances female creativity and diversity in audiovisual, with a sustainable model that ensures medium- an long term growth. We would love for you to be part of this unique community projetc, where audiovisual technology merges with ART, professional collaboration and support.   With the fund's support, we will be able to: -Develop the complete platform (web & app)  - Launch recruitment and training programs for active professionals - Create high-value cultural content that connects and strengthens the creative community - Consolidate a network of legal, financial and support for our user's projets.   We are excited to present FEMSTAGE and explore how this fund can help us build a stable, high-level professional female community in audiovisual industry, with tangible and measurable impact.   thank you  Zule Rojas  Founder of Femstage

Vote **Yes** to include or **No** to pass.

7.title: Curate: Akiya Collective
7.description:
**Should the ZAO fund this project?**

**Project:** Akiya Collective
**Link:** [View Project](https://artizen.fund/index/p/akiya-collective)

our project focuses on revitalizing abandoned houses (akiyas) in rural japan into art installations, community residencies + living rooms / social commons, and sites of collective inquiry + research. we also operate a social r&d lab focused on how to maintain and improve on public goods and social technologies in the 21st century.  we publish all of our findings in an open-source playbook that anyone can use to: acquire akiyas, run residencies and common spaces, and experiment / contribute to social technologies. our community currently consists of ~1,000 global members, many of whom have leveraged (and contributed to) our playbook in acquiring and renovating akiyas, as well as connecting to other members doing similar efforts.   in the next two years, we are also launching a nation-wide participatory art / research experience, in collaboration with a japanese university (chiba tech), and are creating a docu-series, funded by the us-japan foundation, to scale learnings about how to steward public goods + improve coordination in local societies. previously, we have created participatory / public installations around the world, including one at a historical landmark (harvard square).

Vote **Yes** to include or **No** to pass.

8.title: Curate: DreamStarter.xyz
8.description:
**Should the ZAO fund this project?**

**Project:** DreamStarter.xyz

We've helped 200+ dreamers launch their dreams, cumulatively generating 20K USD in revenue and actively pursuing their dreams - from English Education and Spiritual Awareness to Music Prediction Markets.  DreamStarter will enable musicians and creators to spend more time doing the things they love, and leave the marketing to smart AI Agents.

Vote **Yes** to include or **No** to pass.

9.title: Curate: "Profundo" - Cuerpo, Agua y Territorio
9.description:
**Should the ZAO fund this project?**

**Project:** "Profundo" - Cuerpo, Agua y Territorio
**Link:** [View Project](https://artizen.fund/index/p/profundo---cuerpo-agua-y-territorio)

We are a collective of Mexican women creating community-based processes and network to restore the relationship between local community and the ecosystem in the Yucatán Peninsula. We do this through land-based and underwater facilitation processes with local youth — especially Maya communities — as well as activists, biologists, artists, somatic practitioners, and freedivers creating a multicultural web of local people and organizations.  Our work integrates two complementary models:  -Somatic Apnea / Freediving A methodology developed by Fernanda Coatl, rooted in the practice of “Ocean Dance,” and adapted into community processes within their cenotes. This model uses embodied and underwater experiences to foster deep relational connection with water ecosystems.  -Agile Model for Community Self-Governance A participatory organizational framework that unfolds through dynamics designed to make collective intelligence visible and to decentralize power, strengthening community self-governance and shared decision-making.  We are implementing this process throughout March to June (for now). The funds we will unlock from Artizen in June will primarily support the local initiatives that emerge and become woven into the network during our gathering of organizations and community members. The money is for the ecosystem, not for any personal expences, if you see our website we have a transparency document where we make very explicit where is the money going towards. We just work through Ecology/Economy of Generosity.  We also have a website that we would like to further develop. We plan redesign and improve it so we can create an open documentation platform showcasing the years of socio-environmental processes we have facilitated, share results, and provide online tools for communities interested in applying Somatic Apnea and the Agile Model in their own territories.

Vote **Yes** to include or **No** to pass.

10.title: Curate: Participatory Spatial Music Show
10.description:
**Should the ZAO fund this project?**

**Project:** Participatory Spatial Music Show
**Link:** [View Project](https://artizen.fund/index/p/the-participatory-spatial-music-lab)

Participatory Spatial Music Show is a creator-owned cultural platform where audiences don’t attend a performance—they become the choir that creates it.  Participants sing. Harmonize. Contribute lyrical phrases. Offer voice recordings in advance. During the show, their voices are captured, looped, spatialized, and composed into immersive sound environments. The room responds through spatial audio and reactive visuals, dissolving the boundary between artist and audience. Culture is not delivered—it is co-authored in real time.  Emerging technology functions as coordination infrastructure. AI-assisted tools help transform audience submissions into performance-ready songs. Live crowd capture and immersive sound systems weave individual voices into coherent collective expression.   The initial activations will be unticketed public labs in San Pedro, California, hosted at The Majestic—a community-conscious, place-based collaborator committed to local cultural vitality. These labs prioritize access, experimentation, and fair compensation for artists and technologists while inviting the public directly into the creative process.  This is collaborative cultural production, not isolated output. Each gathering becomes a living system where strangers experience themselves as part of a shared instrument. The music, structure, and emotional arc are informed by the collective presence in the room.  The culmination is always real-world activation: immersive, participatory performances that merge digital coordination with embodied singing and communal authorship.  Participatory Spatial Music Show prototypes culture shaped by the many—where technology amplifies human resonance, not replaces it.

Vote **Yes** to include or **No** to pass.

17.title: Curate: The New Vanguard
17.description:
**Should the ZAO fund this project?**

**Project:** The New Vanguard
**Link:** [View Project](https://artizen.fund/index/p/the-new-vanguard?season=6)

The New Vanguard aligns with the ZAO Fund by transforming contemporary Nigerian portraiture into a decentralized public good. Instead of high-level theory, I am building a physical-to-digital bridge: a high-impact exhibition in Abuja backed by a permanent, onchain archive. This project uses blockchain not as a gimmick, but as the literal infrastructure for cultural preservation, ensuring that the raw emotion and fashion of our generation are documented in a non-extractive, community-owned vault that lasts forever. As an insider with five years of deep roots in the Abuja Photo Festival and subcultures like Capitol Film Fest, I have the cultural capital to turn this project into a collective experience. The New Vanguard isn't just my solo output; it is a collaborative "real-world activation" designed to onboard Nigerian creatives into emerging tech. By documenting our identity in public and sharing the process of onchain archiving, I am providing the community with a blueprint for fair compensation and shared ownership of our own cultural narrative.

Vote **Yes** to include or **No** to pass.

18.title: Curate: The Art Factory
18.description:
**Should the ZAO fund this project?**

**Project:** The Art Factory
**Link:** [View Project](https://artizen.fund/index/p/the-art-factory)

The Art Factory is a creator-owned theatre collective I founded as a theatre creative and music artiste from Abuja and a graduate of the University of Ilorin, Department of Performing and Film Arts. It is independently operated by me and a team of actors, singers, dancers, and stage crew who intend to create monthly stage productions that pays everyone fairly and open real opportunities for young creatives and audiences. Our project integrates emerging technology meaningfully by using blockchain tools through platforms like Artizen Fund to document productions, share behind-the-scenes process publicly, mint performance artifacts, and allow supporters to participate transparently in sustaining african theatre storytelling. We also explore AI tools for directions, rehearsal planning, and accessible creative workflows that support artists rather than replace them. Community participation is central to The Art Factory. Each production involves open auditions, storytelling workshops, youth mentorship, and community meetings where audiences shape future themes. We collaborate with local creatives in Abuja and surrounding communities, building a shared cultural process instead of isolated output. We build in public by documenting rehearsals, budgets, challenges, and outcomes through social media, onchain records, and community screenings. Our model is non-extractive: artists are paid stipends, revenue is shared transparently, and skills training helps creatives grow sustainable careers. The project culminates in real-world public performances; one play or musical per month alongside workshops and community showcases that create accessible theatre experiences for young people and underserved audiences. The Art Factory is not about personal funding; it is about building a fair, collaborative cultural home where African theatre stories live, grow, and belong to everyone.

Vote **Yes** to include or **No** to pass.

19.title: Curate: Dark Rooms  Immersive Experience
19.description:
**Should the ZAO fund this project?**

**Project:** Dark Rooms  Immersive Experience
**Link:** [View Project](https://artizen.fund/index/p/dark-rooms--immersive-experience)

Dark Rooms is a creator-owned immersive storytelling project developed by an independent collective of artists, technologists, and producers working across Copenhagen, Berlin, and Taipei. The project merges moving image, spatial storytelling, and immersive technology to create a multi-user VR installation exploring intimacy, vulnerability, and healing.  Emerging technology is used intentionally and in service of emotional depth. Multi-user VR, real-time gesture tracking, and AI-assisted interaction allow participants to move, respond, and shape narrative space together. The technology enables embodied presence rather than passive viewing.  Collaboration is fundamental to the project. Stories are developed through workshops and co-creation processes with contributors whose lived experiences shape the narrative environments. The project follows the principle “nothing about us without us,” ensuring participants influence the creative structure rather than serving as extracted source material.  The production framework prioritizes fair collaboration and transparent practices. Contributors are compensated for their work and credited appropriately, reflecting a commitment to non-extractive cultural production.  Dark Rooms is also built publicly through iterative development. Workshops, prototypes, and touring installations generate documentation and shared learning that are openly communicated with the wider creative community.  The project culminates in a real-world activation: a touring location-based installation presented at festivals, cultural institutions, and partner venues internationally. Audiences enter a shared immersive environment where narrative unfolds through movement and interaction, transforming spectators into participants.  By combining immersive technology, collective authorship, and public exhibition, Dark Rooms aligns closely with ZAO’s vision of collaborative, creator-owned cultural production.

Vote **Yes** to include or **No** to pass.

20.title: Curate: Memethology
20.description:
**Should the ZAO fund this project?**

**Project:** Memethology
**Link:** [View Project](https://artizen.fund/index/p/memethology)

Memethology meets all six of ZAO's eligibility requirements: Creator-owned and independently operated ✓ — Memethology Inc., fully founder-controlled. Integrates emerging technology meaningfully ✓ — blockchain artifacts (minted on Ethereum), AI-powered card creation pipeline, on-chain coordination. Active community participation ✓ — 100+ active Telegram members, 1K Substack subscribers, global player community across 4 continents. Builds in public ✓ — Substack documents the project openly; card drops are shared publicly with narrative context. Non-extractive framework ✓ — Artizen's transparent matching model; no extractive intermediaries. Public-facing real-world activation ✓ — ETHDenver 2026 (every attendee received a booster pack); Prev: Devcon partnerships (twice), Edge City collaboration, Featured on Bankless. Memethology is a trading card game documenting real moments at the frontier of technology — AI, crypto, longevity — as MTG-quality collectible cultural artifacts. Founder Colton Orr (Spider-Man PS4/PS5 at Insomniac Games; Creative Director at Gitcoin, $500K raised for open-source funding). 400+ cards. 4 continents. Tens of thousands in circulation.

Vote **Yes** to include or **No** to pass.
